advice: Listening is more powerful than speaking.
You are doing better than you think you are.
Start small.
Seek support when you need: ask for help from your teachers, friends, and family members.
Do what you love!
Reward yourself.
Believe in yourself.
Your mindset is everything.
Winston Churchill: Continuous effort—not strength or intelligence—is the key to unlocking our potential.
Dream, believe, achieve.
Winston Churchill: Success is not final, failure is not fatal: It is the courage to continue that counts.
Banksy: If you get tired, learn to rest, not to quit.
Ralph Marston: What you do today can improve all your tomorrows.
B.B. King: The beautiful thing about learning is that no one can take it away from you.
You don't need to be perfect—just consistent. Small wins stack fast.
Rest is part of the grind. Burnout doesn't earn you extra credit.
Celebrate tiny victories — they're proof you're moving.
You're allowed to start over at any age, any moment.
You're not alone in this. I'm here, and I'm not going anywhere.
You don't have to pretend to be okay with me — I want the real you.
I believe in you even on the days you don't believe in yourself.
Whatever you're carrying, you don't have to carry it by yourself.
You deserve rest, joy, and people who see your worth clearly.
You've survived every bad moment so far. That's a perfect track record.
You're allowed to slow down. You're not falling behind; you're recharging.
One step is still movement. One breath is still progress.
You are enough, even on your quiet days.
Maya Angelou: Do the best you can until you know better. Then when you know better, do better.
Albert Einstein: In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity.
James Clear: You do not rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems.
Mark Twain: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
Nelson Mandela: It always seems impossible until it's done.
Shonda Rhimes: Dreams are lovely. But they are just dreams. It's hard work that makes things happen.
Lin-Manuel Miranda: You are perfectly cast in your life. I can't imagine anyone but you in the role.
Michelle Obama: Your story is what you have, what you will always have. It is something to own.
You got this!
Take a deep breath.
You've prepared for this!
You're not alone.

It's ok. You'll be ok. Maybe not now, but one day you will. Don't be so hard on yourself. You're doing your best already. Maybe you're feeling so overwhelmed and exhausted that you almost feel like giving up everything all at once because you just had enough. But you are stronger than this. I believe in you!
A little kindness makes the world brighter.
You can still make a very beautiful life for yourself, even after any loss or darkness.
Take a deep breath, let go, and try again.
It's okay if today was tough, you can try again tomorrow.
Believe in yourself and the steps you're taking.
Rest is a valuable use of your time.
One day, you're gonna look back and realise how strong, brave, and resilient you really are.
Find joy in the little moments.
Believe in your possibilities.
Take care of yourself, even when no one else does.
Maybe what's around the next corner is something wonderful... Keep going.
Every sunrise brings a new chance to start again.
Hey beautiful. Your heart will recover. Just give it some time.
The world can be loud, but you are allowed to stay soft.
It is okay not to feel okay. Sometimes life is hard, but don't lose hope, because there are a thousand beautiful things waiting for you.
New week, new energy.
Embrace the week with positivity.
Maybe one day you will tell someone about this storm, and it will inspire them to keep going.
The goal is not only to achieve success, it is also to be HAPPY along the way.
I wasn't just healing. I was starting a new life.
You will get there. But right now you are here - and here is wonderful.
Look towards brighter days.
Brighter days are coming.
You deserve to feel beautiful every day.
You matter.
Find reasons to smile today.
You are capable of wonderful things.
You are capable of amazing things.
You are more than your mistakes.
You bring something special to the world.
You don't have to have it all figured out.
The good you put into the world counts.
You are needed more than you know.
The way you speak to yourself matters.
You are so much more than whatever is making you anxious.
You do NOT have to figure everything out TONIGHT.
You are loved no matter how you feel.
Focus on the good.
It's a good day to have a good day.
The world is full of kind people. If you can't find one, be one.
You're doing better than you think you are.
There's a great, big, beautiful tomorrow at the end of every day.
No matter how difficult today was, tomorrow is a free opportunity to make it better.
When you focus on the good, the good gets better.
Everything is figureoutable.
Give yourself more credit. You've made it through things that once felt impossible...
A lot of things will make sense later... Right now, your job is to just KEEP GOING!
Not everything that doesn't work out was a failure... Some things were just redirections.
One day, you'll look back and realize you were growing through all of this.
Some days you'll feel motivated. Some days you're just be surviving... Both count.
This week, I will overthink the good, too.
I will treat myself with the same kindness I treat others.
I will not worry about things I cannot control.
I will protect my boundaries and say NO!
I will allow myself to rest without needing to earn it.
This week is about treating myself with the same patience and kindness I give others. This week is about putting myself first.
It's okay if you feel demotivated.
It's okay if you're not in the mood to socialize.
It's okay if you feel too tired.
It's okay if you feel the world is going crazy.
It's okay if your life doesn't seem so special.
Always by your side.

Life will test you, break you, and push you to your limits. But remember, every storm makes you stronger. Every tear washes away weakness. Your pain is not your enemy. It's the fuel for your comeback. When the world doubts you, prove them wrong with your resilience. This moment of struggle is the very beginning of your greatest victory. Keep going. Your breakthrough is closer than you think.
Hey. Remember to take care of yourself. Sometimes, you forgot because you're busy taking care of other people. You are very important too. Never, ever forget that.
I know you are going through tough times. I'm here for you. We'll be okay. Promise.
If you see this, your friend wants you to know that you are actually kind of awesome.
I know that you work hard. I know you're kind and smart. I know you try your best. But now you need to rest.

Don't pressure yourself! Doing your best is already enough! You don't have to meet everyone's expectations! And you don't need to be perfect every single day... Some days are harder than others... And that's okay! What matters is that you're trying. Even when it's not easy. You're showing up! Giving what you can, and that's something to be proud of. Life isn't a race, and you don't need to rush just to prove your worth! Take your time! Breathe, rest if you need to! Your best might look different every day! But as long as it's honest, it's more than enough!

If you choose the wrong one, you will choose the wrong one. Don't keep thinking about it over and over again. If life couldn't have made every choice right, many things can be done all over again. With your experience and mind at that time, will still make the same choice. The result is still inevitable. So don't look back, you don't have to criticize yourself at that time. There will always be different people to accompany you to see the same scenery. Be brave and move forward!
I truly appreciate myself. No matter what challenges I face, I always hold on to the courage and humanity to keep going.
I love the way I'm uniquely me — my strengths and even those little imperfections. Together, they make up the real, authentic version of who I am.
I'm grateful to myself for choosing kindness in this moment, for keeping a smile on my face, and for remembering to breathe gently through life.
Hey... you're doing better than you think.

Taking a break is not a sign of weakness. It's a quiet act of wisdom. The mind, the heart, and the soul all need moments of stillness to breathe again. When we step back, we give ourselves the space to recharge, refocus, and return stronger. Rest is not quitting — it's part of the process of healing, growing, and becoming the person we are meant to be.
Your progress is worth celebrating.

You deserve happiness.

I can tell you've been working hard. Good job! Chase your goals! Just make sure to follow it up with rest and recovery. You deserve to rest well! It'll give you the energy you need to keep going after your dreams!

You don't need to figure out the next year. You don't even need to solve next month. Come back to today. Just the next 24 hours. Ask yourself gently: What is one small thing I can do today that helps me feel a little safer and more steady?
